have h : P.initial.balance p ≥ 0 := by
    simp [Factorization.balance, Factorization.sum, Params.initial]
    <;>
    (try positivity) <;>
    (try {
      -- Use a contradiction approach if necessary
      exfalso
      -- Since p ≤ L and p is prime, we may need to adjust the proof strategy
      -- Depending on the exact definition of the factorization, the balance could be zero or positive

      simp_all [Multiset.count]
      <;>
      norm_num
      <;>
      linarith
    }) <;>
    (try {
      -- If p is a prime less than or equal to L, it might not even appear in the initial factorization
      -- So its balance could be zero
      simp_all [Nat.Prime.ne_zero, Nat.Prime.pos]
      <;>
      norm_num
      <;>
      linarith
    })
  -- Since the actual proof of h is straightforward and the result is non-negative, we can directly use it
  have h₁ : (P.initial.balance p : ℝ) ≥ 0 := by exact_mod_cast h
  have h₂ : ((-P.M : ℝ) * Real.log P.n - (P.M : ℝ) * (P.L : ℝ) ^ 2 * (primeCounting P.n)) ≤ 0 := by
    have h₃ : (Real.log P.n : ℝ) ≥ 0 := by
      apply Real.log_nonneg

          have h₅ : P.L ≥ 1 := by linarith [P.hL_pos]
          have h₆ : P.n > P.L * P.L := P.hL
          nlinarith
        linarith
      })
    have h₄ : ((P.M : ℝ) : ℝ) ≥ 0 := by exact_mod_cast Nat.zero_le P.M
    have h₅ : ((P.L : ℝ) : ℝ) ≥ 0 := by exact_mod_cast Nat.zero_le P.L
    have h₆ : ((primeCounting P.n : ℕ) : ℝ) ≥ 0 := by exact_mod_cast Nat.zero_le _
    have h₇ : ((P.M : ℝ) * (P.L : ℝ) ^ 2 * (primeCounting P.n : ℝ)) ≥ 0 := by positivity
    have h₈ : ((-P.M : ℝ) * Real.log P.n) ≤ 0 := by
      have h₉ : (P.M : ℝ) ≥ 0 := by exact_mod_cast Nat.zero_le P.M
      have h₁₀ : (Real.log P.n : ℝ) ≥ 0 := by

            have h₁₃ : P.n > P.L * P.L := P.hL

          linarith

    nlinarith
